query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key summary:

**Is approval still falling?** No longer — both pollsters showed the decline **reversed with a rebound in early July 2026** after a steep late-May-through-June slide.

**Gallup Korea:** Bottomed at **51% approval / 41% disapproval** (June 24–26, lowest since inauguration), then rebounded to **54% / 36%** (June 30–July 2). **No dead cross** — approval always led.

**Realmeter:** Fell for six straight weeks from ~60.5% (mid-May) to **46.5% approval / 49.5% disapproval** (June 22–26) — a **confirmed dead cross** (first inversion ~June 22). It edged up to **47.0% / 49.2%** in early July, but disapproval still leads.

**Dead cross verdict:** YES in Realmeter, NO in Gallup (Realmeter runs ~5–7pp lower).

**Drivers of the decline:** (1) National Election Commission ballot-mishandling controversy from the June 3 local elections; (2) economic concerns — stock market decline, weak won/high forex, inflation, housing instability; (3) earlier "excessive welfare/spending" concerns. The early-July rebound was credited to three mega-regional investment project announcements.

**Party support:** In Realmeter, DP (43.0%) and PPP (40.3%) nearly tied by early July, with PPP hitting its highest level of Lee's tenure; Gallup still showed a wider DP lead (41% vs 26%).

key_facts: Gallup Korea (June 24-26 survey): Lee approval 51%, disapproval 41% — lowest since inauguration, down 6pp; first time Gallup disapproval exceeded 40%. No dead cross.
Gallup Korea (June 30-July 2 survey, released July 3): approval rebounded to 54% (+3pp), disapproval fell to 36% (-5pp) — an 18pp gap; decline reversed.
Realmeter (June 22-26 survey, released June 29): approval 46.5%, disapproval 49.5% — sixth consecutive weekly decline and a confirmed dead cross (disapproval > approval).

Realmeter (June 29-July 3 survey, released July 6): approval rebounded slightly to 47.0% (+0.5pp), disapproval 49.2% (-0.3pp) — first rebound after 7 weeks, but dead cross persists (2.2pp gap, within margin of error).
Realmeter decline trajectory: mid-May ~60.5% -> early June 55.2% -> June wk2 51.5% -> June wk3 46.7% -> June wk4 46.5%.
Realmeter first inversion (disapproval exceeding approval) occurred around June 22, 2026 — first such dead cross since inauguration.

Drivers of decline (both pollsters): National Election Commission ballot-mishandling controversy from June 3 local elections; economic concerns (stock market decline, weak won/high forex, inflation, housing instability); earlier 'excessive welfare and spending' concerns cited in May.
Early-July rebound driver: announcement of three mega-regional industrial investment projects (Honam, Chungcheong, Yeongnam) boosting economic-performance assessments.
Party support Gallup early July: Democratic Party 41%, People Power Party 26%.
Party support Realmeter early July: Democratic Party 43.0% (+2.0pp), People Power Party 40.3% (-1.7pp) — nearly tied; PPP reached highest level since Lee took office in late June.
Realmeter runs roughly 5-7 percentage points lower than Gallup Korea, explaining why the dead cross appears in Realmeter but not Gallup.
